sourceRef.citation: Anushri Banerji, Amol Rokde, Kajal K. Jadav et al. 2026

statement.summary: Radio collars are pivotal tools for tracking the movement, behaviour, and ecology of free-ranging wildlife. They can help understand patterns of home range, migration, habitat use, social interactions, mortality causes, and responses to environmental change. (Cagnacci et al., 2010).

proposer: Anushri Banerji, Amol Rokde, Kajal K. Jadav

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom (radio collars as pivotal tracking tools) is empirically validated in established literature (Cagnacci et al., 2010) but the 2026 date and title reference 'technology' suggest novel methodological or integrative claims under development. The core wildlife ecology claim is TRUE; the provisional status reflects potential algorithmic or AI-augmented innovations not yet peer-reviewed in final form.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 時間を積分し、足跡の勾配に消えゆく距離を重ねると、野生の物語が浮かぶ。
